what_it_does: React canvas component for creating and editing ConceptV deliverables (slide decks, brochures, widgets, wizards). It manages a doc list, new-doc drafting with optional Vi AI generation, an editing workspace for pages and sections, themes, layouts, a candidate gallery, a variables rail, a suggestions rail, undo/redo, drag-and-drop reordering, and a save-as-template flow.
observations: Built-in DOC_TYPES are mixed with registry-driven custom doc types via useExtraDocTypes.
Widget and wizard docs branch to window.WidgetBuilder and window.WizardBuilder instead of the generic editor.
The file relies heavily on window globals: WS_AI, CV_AI, WS_BLOCKS, WS_LAYOUTS, WS_THEMES, WS_MOTIFS, __cvRenderType, CV_REGISTRY, etc.
A central runGallery function drives Vi candidate generation for transforms, layout proposals, block alternates, and insertions.
Per-field Vi regeneration is wired through window.WS_FIELD and routes back into runGallery.
VariablesRail supports manual variable creation, live reference counts, and a Vi audit that proposes new variables.
Save-as-template uses WS_AI.extractTemplate and shows a materialized preview before saving.
Undo/redo are implemented by JSON-stringifying the whole doc into snapshot stacks.
SlideFrame scales a 1280px slide to its container using a ResizeObserver.
Most sections render through window.__cvRenderType; embedWidget and embedWizard use their own renderers.
Inline style objects are used extensively alongside CSS class names.
WSErrorBoundary is hoisted from window but never rendered; BlockMenuButton is defined but unused.
standouts: Tight coupling to a global plugin ecosystem via window.* modules.
Single-file orchestration of AI drafting, candidate gallery, variables, templates, themes, and layout system.
Registry + built-in doc type fusion in the picker.
Conditional React hook call in VariablesRail when window.useWSVars is present.
viDraft references a `doc` variable that is not in its lexical scope.
conventions: React functional components with useState/useEffect/useMemo/useRef aliased with _w suffix.
Window-global module discovery instead of ES imports.
Immutable updates via spread and array slicing.
JSON.stringify/parse used for deep clone and undo snapshots.
Async AI helpers use try/catch and window.dsaToast for feedback.
Event handlers named onX or handleX.
Inline style objects for layout, CSS classes for theme tokens.
concerns: Many runtime dependencies on window.* globals with no local fallbacks, making the file brittle if modules are absent.
Undo/redo serializes the entire doc on every change, which may be slow and stores only the last 30 snapshots.
viDraft uses `doc?.type` but `doc` is not defined in its scope, risking a ReferenceError.
VariablesRail conditionally calls a React hook, violating the Rules of Hooks.
parseJsonLoose can greedily match the wrong JSON object inside prose responses.
WSErrorBoundary and BlockMenuButton appear to be dead code.
Heavy inline styling makes theming and maintenance harder.
Some async paths swallow errors silently.
notes: This is the main Workshop canvas that wires user composition, Vi AI assistance, workspace variables, templates, and a global block/layout/theme registry into one large React component.
questions: Where are the window.* modules defined and what exact contracts do they guarantee?
Is the `doc` reference in viDraft a closure bug, a missing parameter, or an intended global?
How are custom registry doc types instantiated when picked, since onPick passes a fallback type plus the registry type but startDraft ignores the registry object?
What is the expected shape and lifecycle of CV_REGISTRY entries?
Are WSErrorBoundary and BlockMenuButton intended for future use or leftover code?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a visual canvas for composing real deliverables, which it does, but it also absorbs AI assistance, variables, templates, and registry extensibility, making it a broad orchestration surface rather than a pure canvas.
apparent_themes: Registry-driven extensibility over hard-coded document types.
Single-source-of-truth doc model with immutable updates.
Progressive disclosure through panels, galleries, and suggestions.
intention_signals: The file is trying to be the central cockpit where a user can start any deliverable, get Vi-generated drafts and alternatives, refine content block-by-block, manage reusable variables and templates, and export, all without leaving the canvas.
novelty: The fusion of a built-in doc-type picker with a live registry of user/Vi-authored doc types, plus a unified candidate gallery that serves transforms, layout proposals, block alternates, and field regen through one streaming mechanism.
contribution: It contributes a concrete, end-to-end user-facing orchestration layer for document composition: state management for docs/pages/sections, AI drafting and refinement UX, variables and template extraction UI, and integration points for block/layout/theme registries.
summary_for_embedding: Workshop.jsx is a React canvas component that lets users create and edit ConceptV deliverables using built-in and registry-driven doc types, with Vi AI drafting, block-level refinement, workspace variables, templates, themes, and drag-and-drop page and section editing.
intention_for_embedding: It was built to provide a single visual workspace where users and Vi collaboratively compose, iterate, and templatize real marketing and product deliverables.

symbols.does: Subscribes to CV_REGISTRY and returns user/vi-authored doc types.
symbols.description: React hook that listens to window.CV_REGISTRY changes and filters entries at layer 'doc' with provenance 'user' or 'vi' so custom doc types appear in the picker.

symbols.does: Main canvas component for composing deliverables.
symbols.description: Top-level React component managing list and edit views, doc creation, AI drafting, page and section editing, candidate gallery, variables, suggestions, undo/redo, and save-as-template flows.

symbols.does: Closes the transform menu on document click.
symbols.description: Event handler attached inside a useEffect to close the whole-doc transform menu when clicking anywhere on the document.

symbols.does: Extracts variables from a doc and opens the template preview modal.
symbols.description: Async function that asks window.WS_AI.extractTemplate to find template variables, then sets templatePreview state, falling back to a plain copy if extraction fails.

symbols.does: Persists the extracted template to storage.
symbols.description: Function called from the template preview modal that packages the doc, variables, and name and passes them to saveTemplate.

symbols.does: Deep-clones an object via JSON round-trip.
symbols.description: Utility that serializes and parses an object to produce a deep copy, used for template fallback snapshots.

symbols.does: Opens the candidate gallery and streams Vi-generated options.
symbols.description: Async function that sets gallery state to busy and calls window.WS_AI.generateCandidatesStream, appending each candidate and marking done on completion.

symbols.does: Applies a chosen candidate to the doc or field.
symbols.description: Gallery callback that either invokes a field-level onPickOverride or applies a diff to the active doc via window.WS_AI.applyDiff.

symbols.does: Adds more candidates or regenerates the gallery with a hint.
symbols.description: Async function that either appends additional candidates or clears and regenerates options based on a user-provided refinement hint.

symbols.does: Routes a whole-doc transform into the candidate gallery.
symbols.description: Function that closes the transform menu and invokes runGallery with either a direct engine target or a doc.transform instruction.

symbols.does: Handles keyboard shortcuts in edit view.
symbols.description: Keydown handler for undo, redo, duplicate page, arrow navigation between slides, and Escape to clear selection.

symbols.does: Initiates a new document draft of a given type.
symbols.description: Sets draftType and resets draft brief, kind, and system state so the NewDocPanel appears.

symbols.does: Creates a blank or AI-drafted document.
symbols.description: Async function that builds a new doc id, calls Vi drafting for decks and brochures or widget and wizard helpers, then saves the doc and switches to edit view.

symbols.does: Opens an existing document for editing.
symbols.description: Sets docId, switches view to edit, resets page, selection, and variation state, and clears undo and redo stacks.

symbols.does: Returns to the document list.
symbols.description: Resets view to list and clears docId and selected section.

symbols.does: Applies an updater to the current doc and saves it.
symbols.description: Wrapper that calls saveDoc with the result of an updater function or object, guarding when no doc is active.

symbols.does: Reverts the doc to the previous snapshot.
symbols.description: Pops the current snapshot to redoStack and restores the prior snapshot via saveDoc, ignoring the resulting history change.

symbols.does: Re-applies a previously undone snapshot.
symbols.description: Pops the next snapshot from redoStack and restores it via saveDoc.

symbols.does: Replaces a section on a specific page.
symbols.description: Immutable updater that maps over pages and sections to write a new section object at the given indices.

symbols.does: Deletes a section from a page.
symbols.description: Immutable updater that filters out the section at secIdx and clears selection.

symbols.does: Inserts a new section of a given kind at an index.
symbols.description: Creates a section from window.WS_BLOCKS defaults and splices it into the page's sections array.

symbols.does: Reorders sections within a page.
symbols.description: Removes the section at fromIdx and inserts it at toIdx, then updates the selected index.

symbols.does: Clones a section and inserts it after the original.
symbols.description: Creates a copy with a new id and duplicated data, then splices it into the page.

symbols.does: Toggles the locked flag of a section.
symbols.description: Immutable updater that flips the section's locked boolean to block or allow edits.

symbols.does: Inserts a new page into the document.
symbols.description: Adds a blank or layout-built page at the specified index and sets currentPage to it.

symbols.does: Clones a page and inserts it after the original.
symbols.description: Creates a new page with duplicated sections and navigates to the new page.

symbols.does: Deletes a page if more than one exists.
symbols.description: Confirms deletion, filters out the page, and adjusts currentPage to stay valid.

symbols.does: Reorders pages in the document.
symbols.description: Splices a page from fromIdx to toIdx and sets currentPage to the new index.

symbols.does: Sets the kind of a page.
symbols.description: Immutable updater that writes a page kind and clears its motif.

symbols.does: Sets the motif of a page.
symbols.description: Immutable updater that writes the motif identifier for a page.

symbols.does: Sets the document theme.
symbols.description: Updates the doc theme and shows a toast with the theme name.

symbols.does: Inserts a section object at a specific index.
symbols.description: Merges provided data with block defaults and splices the resulting section into the page, then selects it.

symbols.does: Replaces the current page with a layout-built page.
symbols.description: Uses window.WS_buildPage to generate sections for the chosen layout and writes them to currentPage.

symbols.does: Resolves and inserts a library payload.
symbols.description: Calls window.WS_resolveLibraryPayload to interpret a dropped or clicked library item as a layout or section and applies it.

symbols.does: Handles library drops onto the slide area.
symbols.description: Parses the application/x-cv-library data transfer payload and routes it to handleLibraryInsert.

symbols.does: Revises a single section via Vi prompt.
symbols.description: Async function that builds a prompt from section kind and data, calls window.CV_AI.complete, and merges the returned JSON into the section.

symbols.does: Produces alternate versions of a section.
symbols.description: Async function that asks Vi for three structured variations and stores them keyed by section id.

symbols.does: Applies or clears a chosen variation.
symbols.description: Updates the section data with the selected variation index and records the active variation.

symbols.does: Populates an empty slide with Vi-generated sections.
symbols.description: Async function that prompts Vi to compose 2-4 blocks based on document context and replaces the page sections.

symbols.does: Renders the new-document type picker.
symbols.description: Component showing built-in DOC_TYPES plus registry extras, with optional TypeThumb previews and provenance badges.

symbols.does: Renders the new-document brief and options panel.
symbols.description: Component collecting a brief and kind or system pickers for widgets and wizards, offering blank or AI-drafted starts.

symbols.does: Renders a scaled slide frame with motif and chrome.
symbols.description: Component that fits a 1280px slide to its container using ResizeObserver, applies a motif layer, and adds page numbers and a brand mark.

symbols.does: Computes slide scale from container width.
symbols.description: ResizeObserver callback that sets scale to width divided by 1280, clamped between 0.2 and 1.4.

symbols.does: Wraps brochure page content.
symbols.description: Simple container component for one-page brochure previews.

symbols.does: Renders layout choices and AI fill options for empty pages.
symbols.description: Component listing available layouts, plus buttons for Vi layout proposals and Vi auto-fill.

symbols.does: Renders sections with add-dividers between them.
symbols.description: Component mapping page sections to draggable section editors and inserting window.WSAddDivider before, between, and after sections.

symbols.does: Sets a dotted-path value in an immutable object copy.
symbols.description: Utility that walks a path of keys, shallow-cloning each level, and writes the value at the leaf; used for engine onEdit writeback.

symbols.does: Renders an individual draggable, editable section.
symbols.description: Component displaying a section's toolbar, rendering it via __cvRenderType or a custom renderer, and supporting drag reorder, variations, lock, duplicate, and remove.

symbols.does: Renders thumbnail strip for deck pages.
symbols.description: Component showing miniature previews of each page with drag reorder, duplicate, and remove actions.

symbols.does: Renders textual outline of deck pages.
symbols.description: Component listing page titles and kinds with drag reorder and an add-slide button.

symbols.does: Renders workspace variables panel.
symbols.description: Component for adding, editing, removing workspace variables, showing reference counts, and running a Vi audit to propose new variables.

symbols.does: Updates variable reference counts.
symbols.description: Callback that fetches reference counts from window.WS_VARS and stores them in local state.

symbols.does: Adds a new workspace variable.
symbols.description: Normalizes the key, creates a variable object via window.WS_VARS.setOne, and clears the add form.

symbols.does: Runs Vi scan for candidate variables.
symbols.description: Async function calling window.WS_VARS.audit and storing the proposed variables list.

symbols.does: Adds a single audited variable.
symbols.description: Normalizes the key and persists the proposed variable, then removes it from the audit result list.

symbols.does: Accepts all audited variable candidates.
symbols.description: Calls acceptAudit for every variable in the audit result and clears the result.

symbols.does: Renders Vi-generated editing suggestions.
symbols.description: Component that loads heuristic and Vi suggestions for the current doc context and runs them through the gallery.

symbols.does: Removes a suggestion from the rail.
symbols.description: Calls window.WS_AI.dismissSuggestion and filters the suggestion from local state.

symbols.does: Renders the save-as-template preview and editor.
symbols.description: Modal showing extracted variables, editable defaults, a live preview, and confirm and cancel actions.

symbols.does: Renders a materialized template preview.
symbols.description: Component that materializes the template with current defaults and shows page, widget, or wizard previews depending on doc kind.

symbols.does: Renders a button group picker for draft options.
symbols.description: Small component used in NewDocPanel to choose widget kind and system or wizard kind.

symbols.does: Renders theme and per-slide motif controls.
symbols.description: Component listing document themes and letting the user change page kind and motif.

symbols.does: Renders a popover block add button.
symbols.description: Component with a toggle button that opens a grid of available block kinds; appears unused in this file.

symbols.does: AI-drafts deck or brochure pages from a brief.
symbols.description: Async function that builds a prompt with available block kinds and asks window.CV_AI.execute to generate pages and sections; references an undefined doc variable for surface.

symbols.does: Returns default empty pages for a doc type.
symbols.description: Utility returning a single page with appropriate kind and optional default hero for brochures.

symbols.does: Returns default widget or wizard extras.
symbols.description: Utility calling window.blankWidget or window.blankWizard with resolved kind and system defaults.

symbols.does: Returns a default title for a doc type.
symbols.description: Utility mapping type ids to human-readable untitled names.

symbols.does: Derives a short title from the draft brief.
symbols.description: Utility truncating the brief to six words and appending an ellipsis if truncated.

symbols.does: Parses JSON, falling back to extracting an object from text.
symbols.description: Utility that tries JSON.parse and, on failure, matches the first object substring and parses it.
